title: Non-record: 10L mixed int5/int6 export reaches ~10.4MB with strong throughput
author: simon-marcus
body:
## Summary

This PR adds a non-record `track_10min_16mb` submission exploring a QLoRA-style compression direction on the strongest valid `10L` leader-core line:

- mixed export precision by subsystem
- tiny eval-time adaptation on a small control-parameter subset

Saved directory:
`records/track_10min_16mb/2026-03-20_LeaderCore10L_MixedQuant_MicroTTT`

## Main Result

Single-seed `8xH100` run (`1337`):

- `final_int8_zlib_roundtrip_exact val_bpb: 1.24271554`
- `Total submission size int8+zlib: 10401310 bytes`
- `step: 12348`
- `step_avg: 48.59ms`
- `eval_time: 1548ms`

This run is valid on both size and eval time, while pushing artifact size far below the `16MB` cap.

## What Changed

- `int5` export for MLP matrices
- `int6` export for attention matrices
- `int8` elsewhere
- tiny eval-time SGD on `q_gain`, `attn_scale`, `mlp_scale`, `resid_mix`, and `skip_weights`
- same validity-safe non-overlapping final eval and temperature search path as the valid `10L` base

## Why It Seems Worth Saving

This submission maps out an interesting point on the size/quality frontier:

- artifact size drops to about `10.4MB`
- throughput remains very strong on the valid `10L` core
- the compression mechanism works mechanically and creates substantial byte headroom
- the current `int5/int6` recipe is still too destructive after roundtrip
- micro-TTT recovers only a small fraction of that loss

So while this is not a leaderboard result, it does show a concrete and potentially useful direction: aggressive mixed quantization can open up a large amount of artifact budget, but this particular export recipe needs more work to translate that byte savings into competitive BPB.

## Included Files

- `README.md`
- `submission.json`
- `train.log`
- `train_gpt.py`
submission_readme:
This record captures the `CorePromotion Experiment 13` run on Modal (8xH100 SXM).

Architecture discovered via autoresearch loop on 1xH100, then validated at full competition scale.

Configuration:
- Layout: `VOCAB_SIZE=1024 NUM_LAYERS=12 MODEL_DIM=448 NUM_HEADS=8 NUM_KV_HEADS=4 MLP_MULT=2`
- Tied output/input embeddings: `TIE_EMBEDDINGS=1`
- Optimizer: `matrix_lr=0.08 scalar_lr=0.04 embed_lr=0.05`
- Schedule: `warmdown_iters=500 warmup_steps=20`
- Batching: `TRAIN_BATCH_TOKENS=524288 TRAIN_SEQ_LEN=1024`

Command:
```bash
NCCL_IB_DISABLE=1 \
RUN_ID=competition_8xH100_run1 \
DATA_PATH=./data/datasets/fineweb10B_sp1024 \
TOKENIZER_PATH=./data/tokenizers/fineweb_1024_bpe.model \
VOCAB_SIZE=1024 \
MAX_WALLCLOCK_SECONDS=600 \
TRAIN_LOG_EVERY=50 \
VAL_LOSS_EVERY=200 \
torchrun --standalone --nproc_per_node=8 train_gpt.py
```

Platform: Modal (8xH100 SXM, basilias workspace)

Key metrics (from `train.log`):
- Training stopped at `7024/20000` steps due to wallclock cap.
- Pre-quant eval at stop: `val_loss:2.0879`, `val_bpb:1.2366`
- Post-quant roundtrip eval: `val_loss:2.0975`, `val_bpb:1.2422`
- Exact printed metric: `final_int8_zlib_roundtrip_exact val_bpb:1.24223589`
- Train time: `599784ms` (`step_avg:85.39ms`)
- Peak memory: `11921 MiB allocated`, `12300 MiB reserved`
- Serialized model int8+zlib: `16111650 bytes`
- Code size: `47686 bytes`
- Total submission size int8+zlib: `16159336 bytes`

NOTE: Artifact is 159KB OVER the 16MB cap (16,159,336 > 16,000,000). Not a valid submission.
NOTE: val_bpb 1.2422 is worse than the baseline (1.2244). Further optimization needed.

Model params: 17,342,176
Quantization gap: 0.0056 (1.2422 - 1.2366)

Included files:
- `train_gpt.py` (code snapshot used for the run)
- `train.log` (exact remote training log)
- `submission.json` (leaderboard metadata)
